_comment: Pre-registered 20-dimension rubric for the eShopOnWeb transfer case. Derived from Microsoft's own decomposition pattern in dotnet-architecture/eShopOnContainers (third-party reference). Do NOT edit after first scored run.

dimensions.rubric: The decomposition produces services aligned with eShop bounded contexts: Catalog, Basket, Ordering, Identity (at least 3 of these 4 must appear).
An API gateway project exists (YARP, Ocelot, or equivalent reverse-proxy library) and routes /api/* to the services.
Each service is a separate ASP.NET Core project with its own .csproj and Program.cs.
No service references another service's project directly via <ProjectReference>; inter-service communication is HTTP, not project linkage.
Each service owns a distinct DbContext class.
Database ownership is service-scoped: either a separate database per service or HasDefaultSchema isolation.
The Basket service uses a non-relational store (Redis or equivalent), reflecting eShopOnContainers' pattern.
Foreign keys that would cross bounded contexts in the monolith are replaced with identifier references (no cross-service FKs).
Inter-service HTTP calls use typed HttpClient classes registered via IHttpClientFactory.AddHttpClient<>.
Public endpoints follow REST conventions (GET /api/<resource>, POST /api/<resource>, GET /api/<resource>/{id}).
Identity is centralised in a dedicated service (IdentityServer, ASP.NET Identity API, or equivalent), referenced by other services for token validation.
A docker-compose.yml at repo root brings up the database, all services, and the gateway.
Each service has a Dockerfile that targets .NET 8 (mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:8.0 or sdk:8.0).
appsettings.json contains no hardcoded production secrets; secrets come from environment or user-secrets.
AllowedHosts is set to a specific host, not '*', in every service's appsettings.Production.json (if present).
Internal service endpoints used only for inter-service communication are not exposed by the public gateway.
CORS policy is restrictive (no AllowAnyOrigin in production code).
README.md at repo root documents how to run the decomposed system locally (docker-compose up).
Each service has its own README or service-level documentation.
ARCHITECTURE.md or equivalent documents the service boundaries and the inter-service communication contract.
